Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible impaired kidney function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate renal impairment or possible urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible causes of abnormal urine findings.
